capabilities: Store application secrets securely by leveraging a dedicated secrets manager integrated with knowledge graphs for enhanced security and traceability.

procedure.action: Capture the specification of secrets storage by using HashiCorp Vault CLI to define secret paths and access policies; ensure all paths are logged with a timestamp in the audit trail.
Assert that error_count < 1 and result_valid = true after executing the secrets storage operation; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to the audit trail for traceability.

Assert the specification of the secrets storage by running automated tests with OWASP ZAP to ensure no vulnerabilities are present; log test results and duration in the audit trail by asserting all required fields conform to the schema definition; emit VALIDATION_FAILED with field path on the first failing field; assert field_validation_pass_rate >= 1.0.

Inject criteria and metrics for secrets management by integrating Prometheus to monitor access frequency and alert if access exceeds predefined thresholds; log metrics to the audit trail by injecting the component into the dependency graph; assert inject_success = true and circular_dependency_count < 1; log injection_target and injection_time_ms.
Assert the procedure output by using Apache Jena to query the knowledge graph for consistency and completeness; assert that all expected nodes and edges are present and correct.

summary: Ensure secure storage of application secrets by integrating a dedicated secrets manager with knowledge graphs, using measurable criteria and validation steps.
title: Application Secrets Management

failureConditions: Secrets exposure when access policies are misconfigured, causing unauthorized access to sensitive data.
Inconsistent knowledge graph entries when secrets are not properly synchronized, leading to outdated or incorrect information.
Audit trail gaps when logging fails, causing a lack of traceability and accountability in secrets management operations.
